question: what does this video say  for capital gains tax, is it being grandfathered or every now under new rules, does that mean if I sell before 30 June 2027 I can still access 50% discount

runs.name: raw_single
runs.answer: The transcript states that the 50% capital gains tax discount has been abolished for new purchases, but existing properties are not fully grandfathered for CGT (unlike negative gearing). Instead, there is a transitional rule: you can obtain a valuation as at 30 June 2027 (or 1 July 2027) to lock in the 50% discount on gains up to that date; gains after that point move to an indexation model. Therefore, if you sell before 30 June 2027, you can still access the full 50% discount on the entire capital gain, because there is no post-valuation gain. If you sell after that date, only the pre-valuation portion gets the discount; the rest is indexed.

runs.name: rag_single
runs.answer: The transcript states that capital gains tax (CGT) is **not** grandfathered. For existing properties held before 12 May 2026, negative gearing is grandfathered, but for CGT it is not. However, the video indicates that the 50% CGT discount still applies up to a valuation obtained on 30 June or 1 July (presumably 2027). It does not explicitly state that selling before 30 June 2027 qualifies for the 50% discount, but it implies that the discount is available until that valuation date. After 1 July 2027, the rules shift to an indexation model. So selling before 30 June 2027 would likely still allow the 50% discount based on the valuation at that time, but the transcript does not provide a direct statement on a sale before that date.

runs.name: rag_all
runs.answer: The video states that for capital gains tax (CGT), existing properties are **not grandfathered** – unlike negative gearing, which is grandfathered, CGT is subject to new rules [1][2]. However, there is a transition: a valuation on 30 June 2027 (or 1 July 2027) will capture the gain up to that point **with the 50% discount** applied [2]. From 1 July 2027, gains going forward are under the new CGT rules (indexation model) [1][10]. The video does not explicitly address selling before 30 June 2027, but it implies that the 50% discount still applies to gains accrued up to the valuation date. Therefore, if you sell before 30 June 2027, your entire gain would be before that cutoff, so it would likely still be eligible for the 50% discount, though the transcript does not state this directly.
